description: Paper-reported metrics plus reproduction-status metadata for selected method families. Used by generate_paper_comparison.py and generate_reproduction_status.py.

litamin2.paper: Yokozuka et al., LiTAMIN2: Ultra Light LiDAR-based SLAM using Geometric Approximation Applied with KL-Divergence, ICRA 2021
litamin2.repo_scope_summary: The repo reproduces the LiTAMIN2 registration front-end and also keeps a paper-like 3 m profile inside the shared benchmark harness.
litamin2.reproduction_status_summary: Paper-like cost terms and profiles exist, but the stored evidence is still short-window ATE under the shared evaluator rather than the paper's full-sequence KITTI RPE study.
litamin2.numeric_comparison_summary: Paper numbers and repo numbers differ in metric, evaluation window, and hardware, so the comparison is directional rather than direct.
litamin2.main_blocker: No repository-stored full KITTI odometry sweep with the paper's reported metric is exported today.
litamin2.next_step: Add full KITTI Odometry runs for the paper-profile variants and export RPE translation [%] alongside ATE.
litamin2.notes: Paper reports RPE (relative pose error), not ATE. Direct comparison with repo ATE requires caution.

gicp.repo_scope_summary: The repo keeps a self-contained GICP-style registration loop with local covariance modeling under the stable evaluator.
gicp.reproduction_status_summary: The implementation is useful as benchmark evidence for the GICP idea, but not as a paper-result reproduction of a specific historical codebase.
gicp.numeric_comparison_summary: The original paper predates the repo's modern public benchmark suite and does not provide a standardized numeric target that can be matched one-to-one here.
gicp.main_blocker: There is no single paper benchmark and hardware protocol to reproduce against.
gicp.next_step: Choose a concrete upstream GICP benchmark target, or keep GICP explicitly scoped as conceptual baseline evidence.
gicp.notes: Original GICP paper predates KITTI. No standardized numeric benchmark reported. Comparison is with the concept, not specific numbers.

ndt.paper: Biber & Strasser, The Normal Distributions Transform: A New Approach to Laser Scan Matching, IROS 2003; Magnusson, The Three-Dimensional Normal-Distributions Transform, PhD Thesis 2009
ndt.repo_scope_summary: The repo implements an NDT-style registration baseline against voxel Gaussian models with a compact optimizer.
ndt.reproduction_status_summary: This path is benchmark-useful, but it is not a direct reproduction of the original NDT papers or of a single modern NDT codebase.
ndt.numeric_comparison_summary: The original papers predate KITTI-style public odometry evaluation, and modern NDT implementations use materially different code paths.
ndt.main_blocker: There is no agreed modern NDT reference setup wired into the repo as the reproduction target.
ndt.next_step: Anchor against one explicit modern NDT codebase and dataset pair if direct reproduction is required.
ndt.notes: Original NDT papers predate KITTI. Modern NDT implementations (e.g., Autoware) use NDT on KITTI but those numbers are from different codebases.

kiss_icp.paper: Vizzo et al., KISS-ICP: In Defense of Point-to-Point ICP, RAL 2023
kiss_icp.repo_scope_summary: The repo keeps a small KISS-ICP-style local-map pipeline that preserves the main idea while simplifying the full upstream engineering stack.
kiss_icp.reproduction_status_summary: The current implementation is close enough for same-contract comparisons, but it should not be presented as a faithful rerun of the upstream KISS-ICP project.
kiss_icp.numeric_comparison_summary: The paper and repo are both KITTI-oriented and ATE-like, but the repo still uses compact internals, shorter windows, and different hardware.
kiss_icp.main_blocker: The public aggregates are windowed runs of the compact pipeline, not full-sequence reruns of the reference implementation.
kiss_icp.next_step: Run full KITTI sequences and publish an explicit deviation sheet against the upstream KISS-ICP implementation.
kiss_icp.notes: KISS-ICP reports near real-time performance (~100 FPS) on KITTI. ATE values are for full sequences; our 108-frame windows will differ.

ct_icp.paper: Dellenbach et al., CT-ICP: Real-time Elastic LiDAR Odometry with Loop Closure, ICRA 2022
ct_icp.repo_scope_summary: The repo implements the continuous-time two-pose-per-scan core, interpolation, and point-to-plane optimization described by CT-ICP.
ct_icp.reproduction_status_summary: The underlying formulation is near the paper core, but the exported evidence is still short-window ATE under the shared evaluator instead of the paper's full KITTI RPE study.
ct_icp.numeric_comparison_summary: Metric, sequence protocol, and hardware still differ from the published study, so current numbers should not be treated as direct CT-ICP reproduction results.
ct_icp.main_blocker: The repo does not yet export the paper's full KITTI evaluation protocol and metric set.
ct_icp.next_step: Add full KITTI Odometry CT-ICP reruns with the paper-reported metric and publish side-by-side results.
ct_icp.notes: CT-ICP reports RPE, not ATE. Continuous-time formulation. Our repo measures ATE on short windows.

ct_lio.paper: Zheng et al., Continuous-Time Fixed-Lag Smoothing for LiDAR-Inertial-Odometry (CT-LIO), IROS 2023 / derived from CT-ICP + IMU integration
ct_lio.repo_scope_summary: This repo combines CT-ICP-style continuous-time interpolation with IMU preintegration as a compact local prototype built from shared components.
ct_lio.reproduction_status_label: Not comparable to a single paper
ct_lio.reproduction_status_summary: The code path is intentionally a custom integration, so its numbers should not be presented as reproduction of one upstream CT-LIO or CLINS paper result.
ct_lio.numeric_comparison_summary: The current results are useful inside the repo benchmark, but they are not direct paper-result comparisons.
ct_lio.main_blocker: There is no single paper-faithful target implementation and protocol behind this path.
ct_lio.next_step: Either keep CT-LIO explicitly custom, or add a separate faithful-track implementation with its own benchmark protocol.
ct_lio.notes: CT-LIO in this repo is a custom integration. Not directly comparable to any single published paper. Reference-based comparison only.
